title: Hierarchical Recurrent Neural Networks for Sparse Distributed Representations in Sequential Data
abstract: We explore the integration of hierarchical generative models with recurrent neural networks (RNNs) to enhance the extraction of sparse, distributed representations in sequential data. Building on the principles of Bayesian perceptual inference and the learning rules described in the center paper, we propose a novel RNN architecture that leverages bottom-up, top-down, and lateral connections. This architecture is designed to perform efficient perceptual inference and learning in a sequential context. We demonstrate through empirical experiments that our model not only captures the temporal dependencies in data but also learns hierarchical, sparse representations that are crucial for tasks requiring high-level abstraction. Our results indicate significant improvements in performance on benchmark sequential tasks, highlighting the potential of hierarchical RNNs in various applications, including natural language processing and time-series analysis.
